replies.author: @theimaginist3310
replies.text:
⁠@ThePrinceofHisOwnKingdomwe have watched actual good movies. That’s why we know trash when we see it. 

Studios don’t listen to the fans anymore. They don’t treat the IPs we used to love with respect and they mock us when we call it out. This isn’t about watching a “hate mill”. This is about doing what fans have always done. Hangin out and having fun talking about stuff. Since we can’t talk about great movies, the least we can do is keep each other’s spirits up by laughing about the bad movies. It’s good for morale and we get a front row seat as the companies who have demonized us for the past ten years finally pay the consequences for their arrogance. They’re finally starting to realize that they need us; we don’t need them.

author: @TK199999
text: Wanda Vision so infuriates me, it should have been a soft allegory about addiction, depression and truly toxic relationship. Where in Wanda's grief it was Mordo (Mephisto could have worked, but since this meant to lead into Dr Strange) who appeared to her and offered her a relief to all the pain  (soft allegory for drugs). In form of parasitizing off Wanda to create the town and sitcom world. The show would have been about Wanda realizing what was happening (with Visions help), but Mordo would appear and torment Wanda. Telling/exposing her to all the pain she feel if the hex wore off, making her start to remember the pain, then while making her forget while feeding off her pain and magic. Agatha would have been a good guy, Agatha in her youth fell to same trap of despair and pain. Eventually making the same deal with the actual Mephisto leading to loss of her son. But realizing her mistake, has spent her nigh immortal life trying to make up for all her past sins. She would have sensed both Wanda's pain and Mordo using dark magic (hinting at the Darkhold), leading her to try and help Wanda. Eventually Wanda would realize mistake and the people she was hurting, breaking Mordo's spell who in turn would be Quicksilver the whole time. In final battle we learn that Mordo was using the Darkhold to gain power. With part of his goal being collecting power to both kill Strange and prepare for when 'He' starts his war. Leaving it vague who 'He' is so think its Kang and guess in reality by Dr. Doom.  Wanda thinks she defeats Mordo capturing the Darkhold. Wanda realizing what she has been tricked into doing to the people of the town, flees with the Darkhold not knowing its corrupting power. Then have Dr. Strange MoM being about Wanda being corrupted by the Darkhold and essentially relapsing. As the Darkhold makes her think she see's a version of herself sacrificing a version her children to Mephisto. Where we learn it was part of Mordo's plan to use Wanda to safely wield the Darkhold and kill Dr. Stange's across the multiverse. You have even have America Chavez being a pawn in Mordo's plan, giving her false memories and Wanda false visions. Leading to the battle we see on that alternate Earth.
